questions.stem:
-- Where is your father?
-- He _____ Shanghai. He will come back next week.
questions.explanation: has gone to 表示'去了某地还没回来'，has been to 表示'曾经去过已经回来了'。根据后面 He will come back next week 可知爸爸还没回来，所以用 has gone to。

questions.stem: My sister _____ the piano since she was six years old.
questions.explanation: since she was six years old 是现在完成时的标志词，表示从过去某个时间延续到现在的动作。play 是延续性动词，可以与 since/for 连用，所以用 has played。
宾语从句两个要点：(1) 用陈述语序（主语+谓语），排除A和C；(2) 主句是过去时asked，从句要时态呼应变过去进行时were doing，排除D。答案B。

questions.stem: I wonder _____ the school bus will arrive on time tomorrow.
questions.explanation: wonder后面表示'是否'用whether/if引导宾语从句。句意'我想知道明天校车是否会准时到达'。that引导确定事实，what/which需要在从句中做成分，这里从句完整只缺'是否'的含义，用whether。

questions.stem: The boy _____ is wearing a blue jacket is my best friend Tom.
questions.explanation: 定语从句中先行词是人(the boy)，且关系代词在从句中作主语(is wearing)，用who。which修饰物；whom作宾语；whose表所属。

questions.stem: It is _____ (danger) to swim alone in the deep river.
questions.explanation: It is + 形容词 + to do sth. 是固定句型，空格处需要形容词。danger是名词，dangerous是形容词，dangerously是副词，dangers是名词复数。选B。

questions.stem: We should _____ action to protect the environment before it is too late.
questions.explanation: take action是固定搭配，意思是'采取行动'。类似搭配：take steps/measures to do sth. 注意不能用make/do/have action。

questions.stem: The movie was so _____ that half of the audience fell asleep before it ended.
questions.explanation: 根据语境'一半观众在电影结束前就睡着了'可推断电影很无聊(boring)。exciting激动人心的、moving感人的、amazing令人惊叹的都与睡着的语境矛盾。

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held a special 'Kindness Week'. The headteacher announced that every student should do at least one kind thing for (9)_____ each day.

At first, many students thought it was (10)_____. 'Why do we need a special week to be nice?' said Jack. But our teacher, Ms. Chen, (11)_____ us that small acts of kindness can make a big difference.

On Monday, I helped a younger student who had (12)_____ his way to the library. On Tuesday, I wrote a thank-you note to the school cleaner. By Wednesday, something strange happened -- I actually felt (13)_____ doing kind things! It was no longer a task; it became a habit.

At the end of the week, the whole school had changed. Students were holding doors, sharing snacks, and saying 'thank you' more often. Jack, who had complained at first, even started a 'Kindness Board' where people could post nice messages. Ms. Chen smiled and said, 'See? Kindness is like a seed -- once you plant it, it (14)_____ on its own.'
questions.explanation: 根据上下文，校长宣布每个学生每天应该为'他人'(others)做至少一件好事。nobody没有人不合逻辑；themselves自己与'为别人做好事'的主题不符；everyone每个人意思冗余。do something for others为他人做事。
下文Jack的话'Why do we need a special week to be nice?'表达了质疑和不屑的态度，说明他觉得这个活动很'傻/愚蠢'(silly)。meaningful有意义的与质疑态度矛盾；necessary必要的也不对；dangerous危险的语境不合。
老师是在提醒(reminded)学生小善举能带来大改变。remind sb. that... 提醒某人。warned警告语气过重；ordered命令语气不符；promised承诺在此不合语境。remind有'温和引导'之意，符合老师的角色。
我帮助了一个'迷路'的低年级学生找图书馆。lose one's way = get lost 迷路。found找到了就不需要帮助了；known知道也不需要帮助；changed改变了不合逻辑。
到了周三，我居然'乐于'(happy about)做好事了。下文说'It was no longer a task; it became a habit'说明做好事变成了令人愉悦的习惯。tired of厌倦、sorry for抱歉、afraid of害怕都与语境反转不符。feel happy about doing对...感到高兴。
善良像种子，种下之后会自己'生长'(grows)。整段话是积极正面的比喻：kindness是seed，plant it之后会grow on its own，表达善举会自发传播扩大。falls落下、dies死亡、stops停止都与文章正面结尾矛盾。

questions.group_passage:
Have you ever heard of 'dark sky parks'? These are special places where people can see the stars clearly at night, because there is very little light pollution.

In many cities today, streetlights, building lights, and car headlights create so much brightness that we can barely see the stars. Scientists call this 'light pollution'. It does not just affect stargazing -- it also harms animals. Sea turtles, for example, use moonlight to find their way to the ocean after hatching. Too much artificial light confuses them and they may crawl in the wrong direction.

To solve this problem, some countries have created dark sky parks. The International Dark-Sky Association (IDA) has recognised more than 200 such places worldwide. In these parks, special rules control outdoor lighting. Lights must point downward, use warm colours, and be turned off when not needed.

Visitors to dark sky parks often say the experience changes them. 'I had never seen the Milky Way before,' said one visitor. 'It made me feel small but also connected to something much bigger.' Some parks even offer astronomy programmes where experts teach visitors to identify planets and constellations.

Experts believe that protecting dark skies is not just about seeing stars. It is about remembering that humans are part of nature, not separate from it.
questions.stem: According to the passage, why are sea turtles affected by light pollution?
options: A. They cannot find food at night.
B. They are scared of bright lights.
C. They depend on moonlight to reach the ocean.
D. They need darkness to sleep well.
questions.explanation: 原文第二段：Sea turtles use moonlight to find their way to the ocean after hatching. Too much artificial light confuses them. 海龟依靠月光找到去海洋的路，人造光让它们困惑迷路。C选项与原文对应。

questions.stem: What can we infer about dark sky parks from the passage?
options: A. They are only found in countries with few cities.
B. They have strict rules about how lights are used.
C. They do not allow any visitors at night.
D. They were created mainly for scientists.
questions.explanation: 原文第三段说 In these parks, special rules control outdoor lighting. Lights must point downward, use warm colours, and be turned off when not needed. 可以推断暗夜公园对灯光使用有严格规定。A文中未提及只在少城市国家；C明确说visitors可参观；D是为公众体验不只是科学家。

questions.stem: The underlined word 'constellations' in Paragraph 4 most probably means _____.
options: A. groups of stars forming patterns
questions.explanation: 根据上下文，astronomy programmes中experts教visitors辨认planets and constellations。与planets并列且在天文学(astronomy)语境中，constellations应该是与天体有关的事物。结合'identify'（辨认）和观星主题，最可能是'由星星组成的图案'即星座。

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: B. How to Build a Telescope
C. Dark Sky Parks: Bringing Back the Stars
D. The History of Light Pollution
questions.explanation: 全文围绕'暗夜公园'展开：先介绍光污染问题，再说暗夜公园的解决方案，最后讲参观体验和意义。C最能概括全文主旨。A只提到动物是文中一个细节；B文中未涉及望远镜制造；D历史不是文章重点。

questions.group_passage:
Last Saturday, Class 3 went on a trip to Green Lake Park for their biology class.

When they arrived, Mr. Wang divided the students into four groups. (19)_____ Each group had a notebook and a camera to record what they found.

Li Ming's group walked along the lake. They saw many different birds near the water. (20)_____ He quickly took photos and wrote down the colours and sizes of each bird.

(21)_____ She found a strange plant with bright red leaves. Mr. Wang explained that it was a rare species that only grows near clean water.

At the end of the trip, all the groups shared their discoveries. (22)_____ Mr. Wang was pleased and said, 'Nature is the best classroom. You learned more today than any textbook could teach you.'
options: A. Li Ming was very excited because he loved watching birds.
B. Each group was asked to explore a different area of the park.
C. Wang Mei walked in a different direction from her group.
D. Everyone agreed that it was the best field trip they had ever had.
E. The students decided to stay inside because of the rain.
questions.explanation: 第19空前面说老师把学生分成四组，后面说每组有笔记本和相机记录发现。B选项'每组被要求探索公园不同区域'承上启下，解释了分组之后各自做什么。

options: A. Li Ming was very excited because he loved watching birds.
B. Each group was asked to explore a different area of the park.
C. Wang Mei walked in a different direction from her group.
D. Everyone agreed that it was the best field trip they had ever had.
E. The students decided to stay inside because of the rain.
questions.explanation: 第20空前面说Li Ming的小组沿湖走看到很多鸟，后面说他赶紧拍照记录。A选项'李明很兴奋因为他喜欢观鸟'承接看到鸟的情节，解释了他兴奋的原因，也引出下文他拍照的行为。

options: A. Li Ming was very excited because he loved watching birds.
B. Each group was asked to explore a different area of the park.
C. Wang Mei walked in a different direction from her group.
D. Everyone agreed that it was the best field trip they had ever had.
E. The students decided to stay inside because of the rain.
questions.explanation: 第21空后面说'She found a strange plant...'说明主语换成了女生。C选项'王梅朝不同方向走'引出新人物Wang Mei，后面的She就指代她，且explain了她为何能发现特别的植物。

options: A. Li Ming was very excited because he loved watching birds.
B. Each group was asked to explore a different area of the park.
C. Wang Mei walked in a different direction from her group.
D. Everyone agreed that it was the best field trip they had ever had.
E. The students decided to stay inside because of the rain.
questions.explanation: 第22空前面说所有小组分享发现，后面老师说'大自然是最好的课堂，今天学到的比课本还多'，属于正面总结。D选项'大家都认为这是他们最好的一次野外考察'是对整次活动的积极评价，承接分享环节，引出老师的总结。E是干扰项（下雨留室内与全文内容矛盾）。
